message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate S6. Write /tmp/s6_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/s6_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -25' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
S6 acceptance (Plan V4): two independent builds hash-match + hash-pinned deps + locked env + documented build + SBOM. Evidence: assess={"versions_pinned":true,"hashes_present":false,"hash_count":0,"sbom_present":false,"build_doc_present":true,"python_pinned":false,"tooling":"pip 24.2 + Python 3.11.10 available at /home/polaris/pipeline-env. NOT installed: uv, pip-tools, cyclonedx-py, pip-audit (grep only matched uvicorn/uvloop as false positives). Note: requirements.lock was originally produced by uv (per header comment) but uv is not present in this env.","gaps":["No cryptographic hashes: 0 '--hash=' lines across all requirements files; requirements.lock (287 pkgs, all ==) was compiled without --generate-hashes. Regenerate the lock with hashes and install with --require-hashes.","No SBOM: no *.cdx.json / *.spdx.json / bom.xml anywhere. Generate a CycloneDX or SPDX SBOM from the pinned lock (cyclonedx-py not installed).","Python version not pinned as a floor: no .python-version and no requires-python (pyproject has no [project] table). 3.11 only appears implicitly in the uv-compile header comment and tool.ruff/tool.mypy configs. Add .python-version and/or requires-python.","Build docs point at the wrong file: README Quick start does 'pip install -r requirements.txt' (hand-maintained, self-described as non-minimal, un-hashed) instead of the pinned requirements.lock. Add a reproducible-build section that installs the hashed lock with --require-hashes.","No hash/SBOM/audit tooling installed in pipeline-env (uv, pip-tools, cyclonedx-py, pip-audit all missing) — needed to regenerate the hashed lock and produce the SBOM.","pyproject.toml has no [build-system] and no [project] table — it is tooling-only (ruff/mypy/interrogate); the project is not pip-installable/buildable as a package."]}, build={"hashes_generated":true,"sbom_generated":true,"build_doc_written":true,"files_changed":["/home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/requirements.hashed.txt","/home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/sbom.cdx.json","/home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/.python-version","/home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/docs/review_readiness/reproducible_build.md","/home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/README.md"],"no_runtime_change":true,"notes":"All S6 supply-chain gaps closed with REAL generated artifacts (no hand-written hashes/SBOM). PyPI network access was available, so I installed uv 0.11.29 and cyclonedx-bom 7.3.0 into an isolated throwaway scratchpad target dir — /home/polaris/pipeline-env was NEVER mutated.

REAL ARTIFACTS GENERATED (not just documented):
1. HASHES — /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/requirements.hashed.txt: generated with `uv pip compile --python-version 3.11 --generate-hashes requirements.lock -o requirements.hashed.txt`. Compiled FROM the existing pinned requirements.lock (not from the loose inputs, one of which — req-stripped.txt — no longer exists) so versions cannot drift; the step only adds hashes. Result: 287 pinned pkgs (identical set to requirements.lock — verified zero version drift via pin-set diff), 5125 --hash=sha256: lines, every one of the 287 pkgs carries >=1 hash (0 missing). sha256: eff7c152c721eeeea043b568e7b92310bbdeb278db61c6af7f7f3b7fc7b69a69.
2. SBOM — /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/sbom.cdx.json: generated with `python -m cyclonedx_py requirements --output-reproducible --sv 1.5 --of JSON -o sbom.cdx.json requirements.hashed.txt`. Valid CycloneDX 1.5 JSON, 287 components, all 287 carry pkg:pypi PURLs + hashes. Regenerated a second time and diffed — BYTE-IDENTICAL (reproducible). sha256: fdfbc55f454f04fa4b6f94fdcc595bd29c32b2cf9a5ca2d4aa10abd48c0a86ce.
3. PYTHON PIN — /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/.python-version = 3.11.10 (exact runtime patch version). Chose .python-version over adding requires-python because pyproject.toml is intentionally tooling-only (no [project]/[build-system] table; repo is run-from-source, not a buildable package) — documented that rationale in the build doc.
4. BUILD DOC — /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/docs/review_readiness/reproducible_build.md (251 lines): documents the pinned Python version, the pinned+hashed deps, exact --require-hashes install command, SBOM regeneration command, the TWO-BUILD HASH-MATCH method (referencing the deterministic-oracle byte-diff precedent at tests/oracle/demo_artifact_diff.py), recorded artifact digests, and an honesty ledger of automated-vs-documented. pip-audit is documented as a manual step (not installed in pipeline-env).
5. README — added a reproducible-build note in the Quick start pointing at `pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r requirements.hashed.txt` and the build doc, keeping the existing requirements.txt convenience path intact.

DOCUMENTED-AS-COMMAND (not committed as artifacts): pip-audit vulnerability scan (§3.2); the `uv export --format cyclonedx1.5` alternative SBOM path; the pip-compile fallback for hashes if uv is unavailable.

NO src/ RUNTIME CODE CHANGED — verified `git status --short` shows only README.md (M), .python-version, requirements.hashed.txt, sbom.cdx.json, docs/review_readiness/reproducible_build.md (all ??); grep for src/ in the change set returned nothing. Changes are uncommitted on branch chore/review-readiness-s6."}, verify={"collection_ok":true,"hashes_valid":true,"sbom_valid":true,"only_build_artifacts_changed":true}. Note: some artifacts may be documented-runnable-commands rather than generated, if this env lacks the tooling — that is acceptable IF honestly labeled and the commands are real.
Ask codex: "Does this satisfy S6 or make defensible progress? Are the dep hashes REAL (not fabricated), is the SBOM well-formed or its generation documented, is the two-build hash-match method sound, and is no runtime code changed? Flag any fabricated/hand-written hashes or SBOM. End with S6-OK or S6-REVISE (+ the gap)."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate prompt and run CODEX to gate S6.

input.content:
You are gating S6 of a code-review-readiness initiative (Plan V4). Answer as a strict reviewer.

## S6 acceptance criteria (Plan V4)
Two independent builds hash-match + hash-pinned deps + locked env + documented build + SBOM.

## Evidence

### assess (state before the fix)
- versions_pinned: true
- hashes_present: false
- hash_count: 0
- sbom_present: false
- build_doc_present: true
- python_pinned: false
- tooling: "pip 24.2 + Python 3.11.10 available at /home/polaris/pipeline-env. NOT installed: uv, pip-tools, cyclonedx-py, pip-audit (grep only matched uvicorn/uvloop as false positives). Note: requirements.lock was originally produced by uv (per header comment) but uv is not present in this env."
- gaps:
  - No cryptographic hashes: 0 '--hash=' lines across all requirements files; requirements.lock (287 pkgs, all ==) compiled without --generate-hashes.
  - No SBOM: no *.cdx.json / *.spdx.json / bom.xml anywhere.
  - Python version not pinned as a floor: no .python-version and no requires-python (pyproject has no [project] table).
  - Build docs point at wrong file: README Quick start does 'pip install -r requirements.txt' (hand-maintained, non-minimal, un-hashed) instead of pinned requirements.lock.
  - No hash/SBOM/audit tooling installed in pipeline-env.
  - pyproject.toml has no [build-system] and no [project] table — tooling-only.

### build (the fix applied)
- hashes_generated: true
- sbom_generated: true
- build_doc_written: true
- files_changed:
  - /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/requirements.hashed.txt
  - /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/sbom.cdx.json
  - /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/.python-version
  - /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/docs/review_readiness/reproducible_build.md
  - /home/polaris/wt/phase-s6/README.md
- no_runtime_change: true
- notes:
  All S6 supply-chain gaps closed with REAL generated artifacts (no hand-written hashes/SBOM). PyPI network access was available, so installed uv 0.11.29 and cyclonedx-bom 7.3.0 into an isolated throwaway scratchpad target dir — /home/polaris/pipeline-env was NEVER mutated.

  REAL ARTIFACTS GENERATED (not just documented):
  1. HASHES — requirements.hashed.txt: generated with `uv pip compile --python-version 3.11 --generate-hashes requirements.lock -o requirements.hashed.txt`. Compiled FROM the existing pinned requirements.lock (not from loose inputs, one of which — req-stripped.txt — no longer exists) so versions cannot drift; the step only adds hashes. Result: 287 pinned pkgs (identical set to requirements.lock — verified zero version drift via pin-set diff), 5125 --hash=sha256: lines, every one of the 287 pkgs carries >=1 hash (0 missing). sha256: eff7c152c721eeeea043b568e7b92310bbdeb278db61c6af7f7f3b7fc7b69a69.
  2. SBOM — sbom.cdx.json: generated with `python -m cyclonedx_py requirements --output-reproducible --sv 1.5 --of JSON -o sbom.cdx.json requirements.hashed.txt`. Valid CycloneDX 1.5 JSON, 287 components, all 287 carry pkg:pypi PURLs + hashes. Regenerated a second time and diffed — BYTE-IDENTICAL (reproducible). sha256: fdfbc55f454f04fa4b6f94fdcc595bd29c32b2cf9a5ca2d4aa10abd48c0a86ce.
  3. PYTHON PIN — .python-version = 3.11.10 (exact runtime patch version). Chose .python-version over requires-python because pyproject.toml is intentionally tooling-only (no [project]/[build-system] table; repo is run-from-source, not a buildable package) — documented that rationale in the build doc.
  4. BUILD DOC — docs/review_readiness/reproducible_build.md (251 lines): documents pinned Python version, pinned+hashed deps, exact --require-hashes install command, SBOM regeneration command, the TWO-BUILD HASH-MATCH method (referencing the deterministic-oracle byte-diff precedent at tests/oracle/demo_artifact_diff.py), recorded artifact digests, and an honesty ledger of automated-vs-documented. pip-audit is documented as a manual step (not installed in pipeline-env).
  5. README — added a reproducible-build note in Quick start pointing at `pip install --require-hashes --no-deps -r requirements.hashed.txt` and the build doc, keeping the existing requirements.txt convenience path intact.

  DOCUMENTED-AS-COMMAND (not committed as artifacts): pip-audit vulnerability scan (§3.2); the `uv export --format cyclonedx1.5` alternative SBOM path; the pip-compile fallback for hashes if uv unavailable.

  NO src/ RUNTIME CODE CHANGED — verified `git status --short` shows only README.md (M), .python-version, requirements.hashed.txt, sbom.cdx.json, docs/review_readiness/reproducible_build.md (all ??); grep for src/ in change set returned nothing. Changes uncommitted on branch chore/review-readiness-s6.

### verify (independent check)
- collection_ok: true
- hashes_valid: true
- sbom_valid: true
- only_build_artifacts_changed: true

Note: some artifacts may be documented-runnable-commands rather than generated, if this env lacks the tooling — that is acceptable IF honestly labeled and the commands are real.

## Your task
Does this satisfy S6 or make defensible progress? Specifically:
- Are the dep hashes REAL (not fabricated)?
- Is the SBOM well-formed or its generation documented?
- Is the two-build hash-match method sound?
- Is no runtime code changed?
Flag any fabricated/hand-written hashes or SBOM.

End with exactly one of: S6-OK or S6-REVISE (+ the gap).

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/s6_gate.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

**Evaluating dependency hash authenticity**
**Identifying missing two-build execution evidence**
codex
The hashes and SBOM appear genuine:

- 5,125 hashes cover all 287 pinned dependencies, with zero version drift and independent validation.
- The CycloneDX 1.5 SBOM was tool-generated, validated, and reproduced byte-identically. No fabricated or hand-written artifacts are indicated.
- Python and dependencies are pinned, and the build process is documented.
- The change set contains no runtime source changes.

However, S6 requires two independent builds that actually hash-match. The evidence shows only a documented method and two identical SBOM generations—not two isolated builds producing the same build-output digest. Record both clean-build commands, environments, output hashes, and the equality result.

S6-REVISE — gap: no executed two-independent-build hash-match is evidenced.

You sent (first 200 of 1104 bytes): {"verdict": "S6-REVISE", "codex_points": ["Dep hashes are REAL, not fabricated: 5,125 --hash=sha256 lines cover all 287 pinned packages, zero version drift vs requirements.lock, independently validate
Common causes: unescaped backslashes in file paths (use / or \\), unescaped control characters, or truncated output. Retry with valid JSON.</tool_use_error>

codex_points: Dep hashes are REAL, not fabricated: 5,125 --hash=sha256 lines cover all 287 pinned packages, zero version drift vs requirements.lock, independently validated.

SBOM is well-formed: CycloneDX 1.5 JSON, tool-generated (cyclonedx-bom 7.3.0), 287 components with pkg:pypi PURLs + hashes, regenerated byte-identically (reproducible). No hand-written or fabricated artifacts indicated.
Python and deps are pinned (.python-version = 3.11.10, requirements.hashed.txt) and the build process is documented.
No runtime source code changed: change set is only README.md + .python-version + requirements.hashed.txt + sbom.cdx.json + build doc; git status confirms no src/ changes.

GAP: S6 requires two independent builds that actually hash-match. Evidence shows only a documented method plus two byte-identical SBOM generations, NOT two isolated clean builds producing the same build-output digest.
To close: record both clean-build commands, their environments, output hashes, and the equality result (an actually executed two-independent-build hash-match).
